bead.title: execute-bead: record fizeau-resolved route in liveness so the watchdog stops false-killing no-pin attempts (D1)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
In the recommended "let fizeau route" mode (no --harness/--model/--profile pin), the worker's liveness record keeps harness/model/route EMPTY for the whole attempt: liveness.SetAttempt is called once pre-dispatch with the REQUESTED (empty) pins and never updated with the route fizeau resolves mid-stream. The progress watchdog (cli/internal/agent/work/watchdog.go:50 phaseEmpty) treats harness==""&&model==""&&route=="" as a wedge; at the 30-min Running budget it cancels the HEALTHY attempt, which trips the consecutive-wedge guard (threshold 2) and parks the bead to operator-attention. This is the dominant false-wedge (16 beads carry needs-human-reason=consecutive_wedge). fizeau v0.14.41 already fixed the routing-side codex false-reject; this is the ddx-side half.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2440 calls liveness.SetAttempt(candidate.ID, provAttemptID, PhaseRunning, profile, harness, model, profile, 0) — the 4th arg (route) is "profile" (with no profile pin it is ""), harness/model are the empty requested pins. fizeau emits the resolved route in-stream as agentlib.ServiceRoutingDecisionData (Harness/Provider/Model), decoded in drainServiceEventsWithRenderer at cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:257 (no-watchdog fast path) AND :388 (drain-watchdog path), but nothing propagates it back to the loop's liveness reporter (set up at execute_bead_loop.go:1116).

PROPOSED FIX (the watchdog is already correct in spirit — it resets when a non-empty route field appears; only the route is never recorded)
1. Add to cli/internal/agent/work/liveness.go: func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) UpdateRoute(beadID, harness, model, route string), guarded on r.rec.CurrentBead==beadID, overwriting only non-empty fields, same mutex discipline as SetAttempt.
2. Add field to AgentRunRuntime (cli/internal/agent/types.go): OnRouteResolved func(harness, provider, model string).
3. In drainServiceEventsWithRenderer (agent_runner_service.go), add an onRouteResolved callback param, invoke it in BOTH "case decoded.RoutingDecision != nil" branches (:257 and :388) with decoded.RoutingDecision.Harness/Provider/Model. Update both callers: service_run.go:294 passes runtime.OnRouteResolved; agent_runner_service.go:150 (runAgentViaService / ddx run) passes nil.
4. Thread the callback from the loop to where AgentRunRuntime is built (execute_bead.go:1666 runRuntime): pass OnRouteResolved through agenttry.AttemptOpts -> Executor -> execute_bead. In the loop near execute_bead_loop.go:2440 set it to func(h,p,m string){ liveness.UpdateRoute(candidate.ID, h, m, p) }. Also fix the SetAttempt 4th arg so route is not "profile".
5. Do NOT change phaseEmpty or LastActivityAt logic: OnTick advances LastActivityAt unconditionally (wall-clock, liveness.go:87-97); resetting the watchdog on heartbeat-advance would DISABLE it (reintroduces the 80-min wedge). The fix is recording the route, nothing else.

NON-SCOPE: D1b/D1c/D1d/D2/D3 are separate beads. Do not weaken the watchdog or consecutive-wedge guard.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestSidecarLiveness_UpdateRoute (cli/internal/agent/work/liveness_test.go): after SetAttempt(bead,...,route="",harness="",model=""), UpdateRoute(bead,"fiz","sonnet-4.6","anthropic") makes Snapshot() report non-empty Harness/Model/Route; CurrentBead mismatch is a no-op; empty args do not blank existing values.
2. Test asserts OnRouteResolved fires once with the resolved triple in BOTH drainServiceEventsWithRenderer branches (no-watchdog + drain-watchdog) before Final — stub a ServiceEvent channel emitting RoutingDecision then Final.
3. Watchdog test: an attempt with populated route fields past the 30-min Running budget is NOT flagged wedged (progressWatchdog.observe returns fired=false); the existing all-empty frozen case still fires.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
